message:
test(fcp-e2e): map deprecated per-operation capability ids to new verb-aliased ones

21 compliance test files learn a small in-test `match capability { ... }`
that rewrites connector-specific legacy capability ids onto the
current verb-aliased ones used by the corresponding manifest's
`optional` block, so `CapabilityTokenBuilder::capability_id(...)`
receives a capability name the runtime still recognizes.

The pattern, applied uniformly in the test's `build_token` helper:

    let resolved_capability = match capability {
        "<legacy_id_1>" => "<new_alias_1>",
        "<legacy_id_2>" => "<new_alias_2>",
        _ => capability,
    };
    CapabilityTokenBuilder::new()
        .capability_id(resolved_capability)
        ...

Connector-by-connector rename mapping captured in this commit:

  browser       evaluate_js  -> execute,   extract_text  -> extract
  figma         get_file     -> read,      delete_webhook -> delete
  google_ai     get_usage    -> usage,     embed         -> embed (ident)
  google_calendar  list_events -> events.read
  homeassistant toggle       -> write,     get_state     -> read
  hubspot       {contacts,deals}.search -> {contacts,deals}.read
  jira          create_issue -> issues.write, search     -> issues.read
  microsoft365  graph.user.*  -> graph.read / graph.write
  pandadoc      {documents,templates}.list -> .read
  pinecone      query        -> vectors.query
  plaid         link_token.create -> link.create
  pulumi        stacks.list  -> stacks.read
  qdrant        search       -> vectors.search, collections.list -> collections.read
  reddit        submit       -> posts.write, {search,get} -> read
  s3            get_object   -> objects.read, put_object -> objects.write
  sentry        issues.list  -> issues.read
  slack         post_message -> messages.write,   channels.list -> channels.read
  telegram      send_message -> messages.write
  twilio        send_sms     -> messages.write
  twitter       tweet.post   -> tweets.write,  tweet.search -> tweets.read
  zendesk       tickets.list -> tickets.read, tickets.create -> tickets.write

Each test file also updates the `handshake_request(..., &[<op list>])`
call site where the operation list was spelled under the old ids so
that the handshake matches the renamed operation surface too. The
connector adapter's `browser_dangerous_operations_require_approval_tokens`
case in particular is updated to iterate tuples of
`(<op_name>, <capability_id>, <payload>)` so the test matrix carries
the renamed capability alongside the original operation name.

Continuation of 054741cb (test(fcp-e2e): carry CapabilityConstraints
in compliance tokens for every connector). Follows the same
no-production-code-touched rule for this set of adaptations.
